title: Make fill_slots operation_id idempotent
goal: Implement a bounded issue #13 idempotency slice so explicit operation_id on fill_slots maps each internal assign_next request to deterministic per-slot replay keys and a stable run_id, preventing duplicate assignments when the fill_slots tool call is retried.
requiredInputs: Current fill_slots loops CommAssignNext with request_nonce=None and fresh run_id when omitted
permissions: Modify swarm communicate tool helper/branch and focused tests
Run cargo fmt/check/test and selfdev build
completionConditions: fill_slots derives deterministic per-slot assign_next request_nonce values from explicit operation_id, preserving retry replay for each slot.
fill_slots without operation_id preserves existing non-idempotent/default behavior and fresh run_id generation.
fill_slots explicit operation_id uses a stable run_id when run_id is omitted so spawned workers remain scoped across retries.
Focused tests prove a duplicate fill_slots operation_id does not advance to additional tasks after the first fill.
cargo fmt/check/test, git diff --check, selfdev build/reload, governance, commit, and push pass.
failureTaxonomy: Changing default fill_slots behavior without operation_id
Reusing the exact same nonce for every slot in one fill_slots call
Breaking assign_next/assign_task operation_id semantics already implemented
Persisting partial fill_slots state outside existing assign_next mutation replay
notes: Keep the slice tool-level and additive. Reuse existing CommAssignNext request_nonce support and avoid introducing a new server protocol variant.
